theme: The office breathes — now make it flinch, glow, and feel the heat.

ideas.title: Bloberto Error Awareness
ideas.description: When any worker enters error state, Bloberto's speech bubble should snap to a worker-specific crisis line — 'Carlos is on fire. Not the good kind.' / 'Sofia found a bug. Many bugs.' / 'Dave deployed something. I heard a noise.' Right now Bloberto drifts through 'Can we circle back on that PR?' while his entire team is PANICKING. That disconnect is wild. The PING_REACTIONS array already has per-worker personality — it just needs to hook into error state detection. WHY: Bloberto is the centrepiece of the office, his obliviousness to crises makes him seem broken, not funny. Reactive dialogue makes him feel like an actual (terrible) manager.

ideas.title: Desk Progress Ring
ideas.description: In the office view, worker characters show no task progress — you must switch to Dashboard to see the progress bar. Add a thin SVG arc ring around each working character's avatar that fills clockwise from 0→100% as their progress advances. A soft neutral circle for idle/done. WHY: the office tab is the 'at a glance' view — it should give real signal without a tab switch. Progress rings are universally readable at tiny sizes. The data is already there: worker.progress is passed into the Office component but never rendered in the floor view.

ideas.title: Stuck Desk Amber Halo
ideas.description: The code already flags workers as 'stuck' when they've been working >2 hours (data-tier='stuck' in the dashboard). But in the office floor view, a stuck worker's desk looks identical to any other active desk. Add a soft amber pulsing border-glow to the desk element when the corresponding worker is stuck. One CSS class, one data attribute. WHY: if someone watches the office tab in a background browser tab — which is the entire point of this dashboard — they get zero warning that Carlos has been stuck for 4 hours. A slow amber throb on his desk says 'something's off' without breaking the visual calm.

ideas.title: Vibe Playlist Name
ideas.description: The footer already shows rotating vibe taglines ('productivity charts are going vertical') and a vibe version string ('v2.71-in-flow'). Add an imaginary playlist name that changes per vibe: Crushing = '🎵 Shipping Season Bops', On Fire = '🎵 This Is Fine (Lofi Remix)', In Flow = '🎵 Deep Work Zone Vol. 3', Slow Day = '🎵 Elevator Waiting Room Jazz', After Hours = '🎵 3am Terminal Focus'. Zero audio, zero API. WHY: the office already has vibe weather text, door labels, floor glow, and greeting tone per vibe. A playlist name completes the sensory world in a single line. It's the kind of thing people screenshot and send to their team.

ideas.title: Per-Desk Completion Burst
ideas.description: When a worker finishes (working→idle), global confetti fires from the top of the viewport — celebratory but anonymous. Add a short SVG particle burst that erupts from that specific worker's desk coordinates in the office floor. Use their role color. Duration ~800ms. The desk positions are already known in the DESKS array (left/top percentages). WHY: generic confetti says 'something happened', a burst from Maya's desk says 'MAYA shipped that, right there.' The office becomes a place where achievements happen at specific locations — not just a pretty background to the real dashboard.

ideas.title: Ping Button on Worker Cards
ideas.description: The PING_REACTIONS system has brilliant per-worker one-liners ('Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.') but is only discoverable by hovering the tiny office character. Dashboard worker cards have zero access to this personality. Add a small '📣' icon button to each worker card (beside the existing ℹ️ history button) that fires a toast with their random one-liner. WHY: the Dashboard tab is where most monitoring time is spent, yet it's entirely utilitarian. The best writing in the product is hidden behind an office hover. One button surfaces it.

ideas.title: Bloberto Hot Seat Meltdown
ideas.description: When ALL active workers are simultaneously in error state, trigger a 'hot seat' mode: Bloberto's speech bubble fires escalating panic lines 600ms apart ('this is fine' → 'IS it fine?' → 'it is NOT fine' → '🧯'), his desk trophy visually tips with a CSS transform, his candle glow shifts red, and the office border pulses error-red. Auto-recovers after 8 seconds or when error count drops. WHY: the on-fire vibe already adds global heat shimmer, but nothing commemorates the specific moment of total team collapse. Bloberto visibly breaking down is the most shareable, screenshot-able moment the product could have — it's the 'everything is fine 🔥' dog meme, live in your browser tab.
